raw_consumer_statement:
To the best of my recollection because I do not have all the communication records, the following occurred over the period XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. On or about early XX/XX/XXXX, I received a billing showing a number of charges on my then credit card account ending in XXXX amounting to {$1.00} on XX/XX/XXXX and another charge for {$1.00} also on XX/XX/XXXX and {$1.00} on the same date, all charges were disputed. ( XXXX of statement is included ) I contacted Citi XXXX XXXX to notify them that my credit card had apparently been subject to a data breach and someone ( hacker ) was attempting to use the card ending in XXXX charging minor amounts to ascertain if the card was viable. Shortly there after, on the recommendation of Citi XXXX XXXX I was advised by telephone to cancel the account ending in XXXX which I did and obtained a new account ending in XXXX. 

On a subsequent billing under account XXXX, the new account, showing a date of XX/XX/XXXX, a {$250.00} charge appeared whichallegedly was made on the old and closed XXXX account. I disputed this charge which is the subject of this dispute ( Copy of statement included ). The XXXX charges of {$1.00} on XX/XX/XXXX and the {$1.00} on said date had been reversed and resolved in my favor. See statements and attached notification. 

When I contacted Citi XXXX XXXX following receipt of my billing for the account ending in XXXX the charge for {$250.00} appeared showing that charge was orignally made on account XXXX, the account which had been closed due to the data breach back in XX/XX/XXXX.
